user_query:
I’m coordinating our weekly organ transplant operations meeting. Using only the files under input/, please prepare the meeting notes, an updated action item register, and two email drafts for distribution.

Inputs to use (and cite):
- input/agenda.md
- input/participants.csv
- input/cases.csv
- input/previous_actions.csv

Meeting date: Use the date stated in input/agenda.md for all date-based logic and to name the outputs.

Deliverables and requirements:
1) outputs/meeting_notes_YYYY-MM-DD.md
- Replace YYYY-MM-DD with the meeting date from input/agenda.md.
- Include these sections:
  - Meeting Date: <date from agenda.md>
  - Attendees: list names from participants.csv where attending == yes
  - Regrets: list names where attending == no
  - Agenda Topics: list the topic bullets from agenda.md
  - Case Updates: for each row in cases.csv, include case_id, organ, recipient_name, surgery_date, status; if labs_cleared == no and/or consent_on_file == no, append "Prereqs Pending: <labs and/or consent>" for that case; if status == scheduled and surgery_date is within 7 days after the meeting date (inclusive), append "Upcoming <7d>"
  - Carried-Over Actions: list items from previous_actions.csv where status is Open or In Progress (id and description)
  - New Action Candidates: show the counts computed from cases.csv (# cases with labs_cleared == no; # cases with consent_on_file == no)
  - Data Issues: list any assigned_surgeon_email or assigned_coordinator_email in cases.csv that do not match an email in participants.csv; if none, write "None"
  - Sources: list the exact input file paths used (the four files above)

2) outputs/action_items_YYYY-MM-DD.csv
- Columns: id,description,owner_email,due_date,status,related_case_id,source
- Carried-over items: from previous_actions.csv where status is Open or In Progress; include each as a new row with:
  - id = CO-<original id>
  - description = original description
  - owner_email = original owner_email
  - due_date = original due_date (keep as is)
  - status = Carried Over
  - related_case_id = original related_case_id
  - source = carried_over
- New items from cases.csv (one row per rule that applies):
  - If labs_cleared == no: description = "Complete required labs for <recipient_name> (<case_id>)"; owner_email = the Lab Liaison email from participants.csv (role == Lab Liaison); due_date = meeting date + 2 days; status = Open; id = N-<case_id>-LAB; source = new
  - If consent_on_file == no: description = "Obtain consent for <recipient_name> (<case_id>)"; owner_email = assigned_coordinator_email from cases.csv; due_date = meeting date + 1 day; status = Open; id = N-<case_id>-CONSENT; source = new
  - If status == scheduled and surgery_date is within 3 days after the meeting date (inclusive): description = "Confirm pre-op huddle for <recipient_name> (<case_id>)"; owner_email = assigned_surgeon_email; due_date = meeting date; status = Open; id = N-<case_id>-HUDDLE; source = new
- Dates must use YYYY-MM-DD format.

3) outputs/email_surgeons_YYYY-MM-DD.txt
- To: a comma-separated list of emails from participants.csv where role == Surgeon
- Subject: "Transplant coordination — surgical updates for YYYY-MM-DD"
- Body must include:
  - A brief opening line referencing the meeting date
  - A summary line with the count of scheduled surgeries within 7 days after the meeting date (inclusive), based on cases.csv
  - A list of those upcoming cases (case_id, recipient_name, surgery_date) and for each indicate if a corresponding HUDDLE action exists in outputs/action_items_YYYY-MM-DD.csv
  - A closing line inviting corrections

4) outputs/email_coord_lab_YYYY-MM-DD.txt
- To: a comma-separated list of emails from participants.csv where role is either Transplant Coordinator or Lab Liaison
- Subject: "Transplant coordination — labs/consents and action items for YYYY-MM-DD"
- Body must include:
  - A brief opening line referencing the meeting date
  - A section listing carried-over items from outputs/action_items_YYYY-MM-DD.csv that are assigned to recipients in this email’s To list (show id, description, due_date)
  - A section listing new LAB and CONSENT items (id, description, owner_email, due_date)
  - A closing line with next-step reminder

Notes:
- Do not invent data; derive all content from the input files.
- Use the meeting date from input/agenda.md both in file names and in date-based calculations.
- Ensure the meeting notes include the Sources section listing the four input file paths above.

Please place all four deliverables in the outputs/ directory as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
